title: French interior minister vows tougher action against illegal raves
content: The French interior minister has vowed to clamp down harder on illegal rave gatherings after tens of thousands attended an authorised event this weekend.
snippet: The French interior minister has vowed to clamp down harder on illegal rave gatherings after tens of thousands attended an authorised event this weekend.

title: 'No strategy' behind Trump's withdrawal of NATO troops from Germany, sources say
content: Trump did not warn allies prior to his abrupt announcement of the withdrawal of 5,000 active-duty troops from Germany. The sudden move came amid an ongoing feud with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, sparked by his criticism of the US' war in Iran, and Washington's strategy.
snippet: Trump did not warn allies prior to his abrupt announcement of the withdrawal of 5,000 active-duty troops from Germany. The sudden move came amid an ongoing feud with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz,

title: At least 10 killed, over 70 injured in Russian attacks across Ukraine in past 24 hours, Kyiv says
content: Kyiv says Russian drone and missile strikes have killed at least 10 people and injured more than 76 others across the country over the last 24 hours.
snippet: Kyiv says Russian drone and missile strikes have killed at least 10 people and injured more than 76 others across the country over the last 24 hours.

title: Robert Fico will back Ukraine's EU membership after call, Zelenskyy says
content: Zelenskyy said they had invited each other to meet. Slovakia's prime minister has backed Viktor Orbán's isolated line on Ukraine, though less fervently than the outgoing Hungarian leader.
snippet: Zelenskyy said they had invited each other to meet. Slovakia's prime minister has backed Viktor Orbán's isolated line on Ukraine, though less fervently than the outgoing Hungarian leader.

title: End of the rescue operation: Humpback whale "Timmy" released in the North Sea
content: The humpback whale that was rescued from a shallow Baltic Sea bay off the island of Poel has been released, the private rescue initiative has announced. However, it is unclear what condition the animal is in.

title: Two flotilla detainees arrive in Israel amid fears for Spaniard Abu Keshek
content: Israel has confirmed that Spanish activist Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Ávila, detained during the raid on the Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters, are now in Israeli territory awaiting questioning. Spain deems the arrest illegal.

title: Two people killed and over a dozen injured in Russian attacks across Ukraine
content: Russia is continuing to bombard Ukrainian cities daily with barrages of drones and missiles as ceasefire talks appear to have stalled in the wake of the war in Iran. Kyiv says its counter strikes targeting Russian oil facilities have been achieving success.

title: France and Germany send firefighters to help battle woodland blazes in Netherlands
content: Many of the fires, which broke out on Wednesday and Thursday, were raging in land used for military training, including an artillery range, in the south.
snippet: Many of the fires, which broke out on Wednesday and Thursday, were raging in land used for military training, including an artillery range, in the south.

title: Hungary's Péter Magyar defends appointing brother-in-law as country's justice minister
content: Magyar campaigned on a promise to clean-up Hungarian politics and restore the checks and balances which had been gradually eroded by previous Prime Minister Viktor Orbán during his 16 years in power.
snippet: Magyar campaigned on a promise to clean-up Hungarian politics and restore the checks and balances which had been gradually eroded by previous Prime Minister Viktor Orbán during his 16 years in power.

title: Trump to raise US tariffs on EU cars to 25%, accusing bloc of not complying with deal
content: Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to the trade deal last July which set a 15% tariff on most goods.
snippet: Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to the trade deal last July which set a 15% tariff on most goods.

title: Arson attack at Skopje synagogue linked to so-called Islamic State, officials say
content: The Islamic State group has a profoundly hostile opposition toward Jews and its ideology frames Judaism as the primary enemy of Islam.

title: Man detained for plotting attack on Dutch princesses with axe to appear in court

title: UK raises terror threat level to severe after two Jewish men stabbed in north London
content: Severe is the second-highest rung on a five-point scale and means intelligence agencies consider an attack highly likely in the next six months.
snippet: Severe is the second-highest rung on a five-point scale and means intelligence agencies consider an attack highly likely in the next six months.

title: Trump launches fresh tirade against Merz after troop withdrawal threat
content: Trump's barrage of criticism comes in response to Merz's comments on Monday that the US was being "humiliated" by the Iranian leadership.
snippet: Trump's barrage of criticism comes in response to Merz's comments on Monday that the US was being "humiliated" by the Iranian leadership.

title: 'We are ready to discuss digital rules with the US but cannot wipe out rules,' EU trade chief says

title: Spain's PM 'was at level one' in scheme to rig public contracts, defendant in graft case says

title: China pushes EU capitals to scrap 'Made in Europe' law or face retaliation
content: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.
snippet: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.

title: Italy says it has filed to be civil party in probe into Swiss bar fire that killed 41
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.
snippet: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.

title: Magyar says EU money to arrive in Hungary 'soon' after meeting with von der Leyen

title: US Hormuz coalition 'not in competition' with France-UK proposal, FM says
content: France says a new US-led coalition to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will complement, not compete with, its own UK-backed mission as standoff with Iran in the vital waterway roils on.

title: World record made in Europe: The world’s longest railway tunnel is being built under the Alps
content: Deep below the Alps, huge tunnelling machines are cutting their way through the mountain massif. The longest railway tunnel in the world is being built at the Brenner Pass. An engineering masterpiece made in Europe.

title: Workers' health isn't the only thing at risk of job strain — the economy is too, study finds

title: Man charged with attempted murder over stabbings of Jewish men in London

title: After Orbán's defeat, the EU faces tough questions about Ukraine's accession

title: Fact check: Did Ukraine's US ambassador steal champagne during White House dinner shooting?

title: UK PM Starmer jeered by crowd on arrival at scene of stabbing of two Jewish men
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is heckled with jeers of "Jew harmer" and "shame on you" as he arrives in north London where two Jewish men were stabbed the day before. The attack, which the police have labelled a "terrorist incident", comes after a series of attacks on Jewish sites in the area.

title: Spain's 'humane migration policy' is 'model for Europe', minister says
content: Spain's Migration Minister defended the country’s migration approach in an interview with Euronews amid criticism from EU leaders on plans to regularise an estimated 500,000 undocumented migrants.
snippet: Spain's Migration Minister defended the country’s migration approach in an interview with Euronews amid criticism from EU leaders on plans to regularise an estimated 500,000 undocumented migrants.

title: Belgium seeks to take control of nuclear reactors in move to secure energy supplies

title: Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire
content: After the Easter truce, Moscow is now proposing a Victory Day parade ceasefire as the Kremlin is concerned over Ukraine’s long-range striking capabilities. Moscow has already scaled down the parade, where for the first time in years, Russian armoured columns will not be rolling on the Red Square.

title: Global press freedom at lowest level in 25 years, Reporters Without Borders report shows
content: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.
snippet: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.

title: The "giant" heads home: After war missions and Maduro capture, a record return

title: Switzerland or Italy? Who covers medical costs after the Crans-Montana fire?

title: EU failed to 'listen' in Sahel, envoy admits as influence wanes

snippet: EU envoy admits Brussels misread Sahel dynamics, warning that military-first strategies and one-way diplomacy have eroded influence as Russia and China deepen their foothold in the region.

title: 'We cannot give Russia legitimacy through sports events', says MEP Ville Niinistö
content: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".
snippet: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".

title: From statements to actions: Here's which European countries are spending the most on defence
content: Military spending by European NATO members last year rose faster than at any time since 1953.

title: Italian woman faces jail in Egypt for adultery as custody battle intensifies

title: Europe Today: Péter Magyar first visit to Brussels and China-EU tensions rising

title: Czechia: A small country with big nuclear energy ambitions
content: Czechia stands out in Europe for its bold pursuit of nuclear energy sovereignty and its ambition to lead in atomic power as it cuts reliance on fossil fuels.
